label: Ashlar Tenement
description: Ashlar Tenement occupies a re-faced section of ancient masonry, its fractured stone courses stitched with blackened brick and riveted iron braces characteristic of Cindervale’s reconstruction. A narrow entry corridor opens onto stacked rooms lit by slit windows, with soot-dark rafters and smoke-scored plaster recording years of coal heat and siege rationing. Interior door lintels carry stamped rental tallies and quarantine notices copied from the city’s license-stones, binding domestic life to the same codified ordinances that govern the gates. A small inner court holds a hand pump and rain barrels set behind iron grating, reflecting the city’s entrenched concern with water security and contamination control.
